objective: Add a governance-owned Local CI Gate profile for local-ai-machine without modifying the LAM repo.
scope_boundary: Design the profile from local-ai-machine origin/main and read-only workflow inspection.
Add a bundled Local CI Gate profile for local-ai-machine using deterministic commands that already exist in LAM.
Add focused tests proving the profile loads and scopes heavier checks to matching paths.
Update the Local CI Gate toolkit runbook profile catalog.
Do not install or modify a LAM managed shim in this governance issue.
out_of_scope: Editing the dirty local-ai-machine shared checkout.
Installing a managed shim in local-ai-machine.
Running environment-sensitive LAM runtime or remote host checks locally.
Cleaning dirty shared checkouts outside the isolated governance worktree.
research_summary: LAM is the next Local CI Gate consumer candidate after knocktracker and AIS. Its shared checkout is dirty and behind origin/main, so the profile must be based on origin/main read-only inspection. LAM has no package.json scripts and uses direct Python, Deno, and bash workflow commands, so the profile should call stable existing commands directly and scope expensive/runtime-sensitive checks by path.

sprints.name: Planning and Scope Bootstrap
sprints.goal: Land trusted planning and implementation scope artifacts before profile implementation.
tasks: Add planning and implementation execution-scope artifacts.
Add PDCA/R and planning cross-review artifacts.
Update governance mirrors so #265 is no longer active and #272 is active.
Validate structured plans, backlog alignment, diff hygiene, and planner-boundary scope.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub issue #272 is open and referenced by governance mirrors.
Planning PR contains only planning/scope/cross-review/status artifacts.
Implementation branch and allowed write paths are declared before implementation starts.
The planning PR does not change runner behavior, profile behavior, or downstream repos.

sprints.name: LAM Profile Implementation
sprints.goal: Add a reusable profile that lets LAM adopt the managed Local CI Gate shim in a later repo-local PR.
tasks: Use direct existing LAM commands rather than creating npm wrappers.
Scope runtime-sensitive or heavier checks to matching changed files.
Add bundled-profile and changed-file scope tests.
Update the Local CI Gate toolkit profile catalog and closeout evidence.
acceptance_criteria: The LAM profile loads with unique check IDs and dependency metadata.
A docs-only or unrelated diff plans only always-on/lightweight checks.
LAM script or workflow changes plan matching focused checks.
Runbook catalog lists local-ai-machine and notes consumer shim rollout is separate.
No local-ai-machine repo files are modified.

specialist_reviews.focus: Map LAM origin/main workflows into a safe Local CI Gate profile without editing LAM.
specialist_reviews.summary: The profile should use direct Python/Deno/bash commands already present in LAM workflows and keep heavy checks path-scoped. Downstream shim adoption is a separate issue.

alternate_model_review.summary: The existing Local CI Gate profile pattern is approved for LAM with the constraint that this issue only adds the governance-owned profile. Consumer shim installation must be tracked separately in local-ai-machine.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #272 may add a local-ai-machine Local CI Gate profile, focused tests, runbook catalog docs, progress mirror updates, and closeout evidence in governance only. It must not modify the LAM repo.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Merge this planning/scope branch so the implementation execution scope is trusted from main, then implement on branch feat/issue-272-local-ai-machine-profile.
blocked_on: planning PR merged so implementation scope is trusted from main

material_deviation_rules: If implementation needs local-ai-machine repo changes, stop and open a separate LAM issue.
If profile commands require new LAM wrapper scripts, stop and re-plan in LAM rather than adding them here.
If runtime-sensitive checks cannot be made dry-run safe, keep them scoped and document CI as authoritative.
Do not alter dirty shared checkouts outside this isolated governance worktree.
approved_by: operator instruction: then we can work on LAM
